question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a configuration payload (e.g., via an exposed actuator or environment endpoint) that sets both blockProperties.isEnabled() and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() to true simultaneously, triggering the IllegalStateException in init() and preventing the importer from ever starting, causing a total halt in transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition or hot-reload mechanism (e.g., Spring Cloud Config refresh) to flip both blockProperties.isEnabled() and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() to true after startup, causing init() to throw IllegalStateException on a subsequent context refresh and shutting down the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the Flyway HikariCP connection pool (setMaximumPoolSize(10)) by flooding the database with long-running connections from the same host, preventing Flyway from acquiring a connection during startup and causing the importer to fail to initialize, halting all transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setInitializationFailTimeout(-1) setting on the Flyway HikariCP pool to cause the importer to hang indefinitely during startup by keeping the database unreachable, effectively blocking all transaction ingestion without any timeout-based recovery?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a TCP-level connection reset or RST injection against the database port to cause dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() to loop indefinitely, blocking the flywayDataSource() bean initialization and preventing the importer from ever completing startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malformed or out-of-range topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp value (e.g., via an exposed configuration endpoint) that causes Flyway migration SQL to fail when the placeholder is substituted, preventing the database schema from being applied and halting the importer startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the importerProperties.getNetwork() return value (e.g., by injecting a network name that case-insensitively matches MAINNET) to force the wrong topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp (1592499600000000000L vs 1588706343553042000L) into Flyway placeholders, causing migration scripts to apply incorrect schema state and corrupting transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network name string that, after the substringBefore/toLowerCase processing in getNetwork(), equalsIgnoreCase-matches 'mainnet' unexpectedly (e.g., 'MAINNET-custom'), causing the wrong hardcoded timestamp to be injected into Flyway placeholders and breaking migration-dependent transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DNS poisoning or SSRF-style redirect of the jdbcUrl resolved by dataSourceProperties.determineUrl() to point to a malicious or unavailable host, causing dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() to block until timeout and then throw IllegalStateException, preventing the importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that connectionDetails.getJdbcUrl() overrides dataSourceProperties.determineUrl() without validation, by injecting a malicious JdbcConnectionDetails bean (via a classpath or service-loader attack), redirecting the Flyway data source to an attacker-controlled database and causing migration failures that halt transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated application restarts (e.g., via OOM or crash-loop exploitation) combined with a slow database that causes dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() to consume the full timeout on each restart, effectively keeping the importer in a permanent startup loop and preventing any transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user inject or manipulate the spring.task.scheduling.enabled property (e.g., via an exposed /actuator/env endpoint) to set it to false, causing the @ConditionalOnProperty to skip @EnableScheduling registration and disabling all scheduled transaction download tasks, halting new transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SchedulingConfiguration is a protected static inner class with no access control, and that the comment 'This toggle exists only to disable scheduling for test execution and shouldn't be modified by operators' implies no runtime enforcement, to disable scheduling via environment manipulation and halt all transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the HikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig) call to propagate a misconfigured or attacker-influenced base hikariConfig (e.g., with an extremely low connectionTimeout) into the Flyway pool, causing all Flyway migration connection attempts to time out and preventing the importer from completing schema setup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed setMaximumPoolSize(10) on the Flyway HikariCP pool combined with a slow or unresponsive database to hold all 10 connections in a waiting state, starving Flyway of connections during migration and causing the importer startup to fail, preventing transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setIdleTimeout(60000) (60 seconds) on the Flyway pool to cause connections to be dropped during a long-running Flyway migration, forcing Flyway to fail mid-migration and leaving the database schema in a partially migrated state that prevents the importer from processing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setMinimumIdle(0) setting on the Flyway HikariCP pool to cause connection establishment latency spikes during Flyway migration (since no connections are pre-warmed), and if the database is under load, cause migration timeouts that prevent the importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the dbProperties.getOwner() and dbProperties.getOwnerPassword() credentials used in flywayDataSource() to be leaked via an exposed /actuator/configprops or /actuator/env endpoint, then use those owner-level credentials to directly corrupt the Flyway schema history table and cause all subsequent migration runs to fail, halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway pool uses owner-level database credentials (dbProperties.getOwner()) to perform a privilege escalation from a read-only database user to owner-level access, then drop or corrupt critical tables used for transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Long.MAX_VALUE or Long.MIN_VALUE as topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp via an exposed configuration endpoint, causing the Flyway placeholder substitution to produce an out-of-range timestamp that breaks migration SQL and prevents the importer from initializing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user set topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp to 0 or a negative value via configuration injection, causing Flyway migration scripts that use this placeholder in WHERE clauses or partition boundaries to produce incorrect schema state, corrupting the transaction ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation on the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp value before it is passed to configuration.getPlaceholders().put(), injecting a SQL-fragment or special character string that breaks Flyway's placeholder substitution and causes migration failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a deserialization or remote configuration injection vulnerability to set blockProperties.enabled=true at runtime after the @PostConstruct init() check has already passed, bypassing the mutual-exclusion guard and causing both block and record downloaders to run simultaneously, leading to data corruption and transaction processing failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() only checks the enabled state at @PostConstruct time (not continuously) to cause a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race where both blockProperties and recordDownloaderProperties are enabled after the check, causing duplicate transaction processing and data inconsistency that halts confirmed transaction flow?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the new HikariDataSource(flywayHikariConfig) constructor to throw an unchecked exception (e.g., by exhausting file descriptors or thread pool resources), which propagates as a Spring bean creation failure and prevents the entire application context from loading, halting all transaction ingestion?
